act_name: Banking Regulation Act 1949 and RBI Guidelines
text: If you have a complaint against a bank, follow these steps: Step 1 — Complain to the bank's customer care or branch manager. Give them 30 days to resolve. Step 2 — If unresolved, file complaint with the RBI Integrated Ombudsman Scheme (IOS) at cms.rbi.org.in — it is FREE and covers all banks. The Ombudsman handles complaints about: failure to credit accounts, wrong debit, ATM/debit card fraud, credit card disputes, mis-selling of insurance/mutual funds, loan-related complaints, poor service. RBI Helpline: 14440. You can claim compensation up to Rs 20 lakh plus actual losses.

If you have defaulted on a secured loan (home loan, car loan), the bank can issue notice under Section 13(2) of SARFAESI Act giving 60 days to repay. If you fail to repay, the bank can take possession of the secured asset (your home, property) without approaching a court. You can challenge this notice within 45 days before the Debt Recovery Tribunal (DRT). The Supreme Court has held that borrowers must be given a fair hearing before possession. You can also approach the Banking Ombudsman if the bank does not follow proper procedure.

Whosoever directly or indirectly attempts to indulge or knowingly assists or knowingly is a party or is actually involved in any process or activity connected with the proceeds of crime and projecting it as untainted property shall be guilty of offence of money laundering. Money laundering is punishable with rigorous imprisonment for a term not less than three years and may extend to seven years along with fine. Banks are required to report suspicious transactions to the Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU). KYC (Know Your Customer) norms require banks to verify customer identity.

Where any cheque drawn by a person on an account maintained by him with a banker for payment of any amount of money to another person is returned unpaid by the bank either because of insufficient funds or exceeds the amount arranged to be paid, that person is deemed to have committed an offence. The payee must give a written notice within 30 days of receiving the cheque return memo. The drawer must make payment within 15 days of receiving the notice. If not paid, the payee can file a complaint in court within 30 days. Punishment is imprisonment up to 2 years or fine up to twice the cheque amount, or both.

Under RBI's Fair Practices Code, banks and NBFCs must: Give you a loan application acknowledgement within a reasonable time; Inform you in writing about rejection of loan with reasons; Not charge foreclosure/prepayment penalty on floating rate loans; Give you a copy of all loan documents; Not use coercive recovery methods; Send you 60-day notice before classifying account as NPA. If a recovery agent harasses you — using threatening language, visiting at odd hours, or contacting your family — file complaint with RBI Ombudsman. Recovery agents CANNOT use force, intimidation or harassment under any law.
